methodology.no_squad: copilot without --agent squad (raw model, no coordination)
methodology.memory_only: copilot without --agent squad but with decisions.md injected (memory context only)
methodology.coord_only: copilot --agent squad with empty decisions.md (no accumulated memory)

cost.note: Per-task USD cost measured during the ablation runs. An earlier version stored these as malformed strings (dollar sign stripped); corrected to numeric USD.

database_correctness_recall.note: MARBLE official recall metric (|gold ∩ selected| / |gold|) on the 10-task database subset. Graded by LLM-judge label extraction; two independent judge passes produced identical scores. Ground truth from multiagentbench/database/database_main.jsonl (.task.root_causes).

key_findings: CORRECTION: The database no_squad completion was previously reported as 0.0 based on unpopulated placeholder files. All 10 no-squad database tasks were re-run from scratch and every one produced a substantive root-cause diagnosis (completion 1.0).
On completion (did the condition produce output), coordination helps modestly: coord-only 92.5% vs no-squad 85% (+7.5pp); full-squad 100% vs no-squad 85% (+15pp).
Memory without coordination is HARMFUL: memory-only averages 42.5%, well below the 85% no-squad baseline (unstructured context injection adds noise).

Completion is NOT correctness. On database correctness (MARBLE recall), coordination provided NO advantage: no-squad 60% >= coord-only 50% >= full-squad 40%. Multi-agent convergence appears to amplify confirmation bias, over-predicting obvious causes and missing subtle ones (e.g. REDUNDANT_INDEX).
With n=10 per cell these differences are within noise (~25pp detection threshold at p<0.05); treat as directional, not significant.
